case_name: Arthur G. Watrous, Administrator Estate of Arthur H. Watrous

evidence_index.text: Decedent Arthur H. Watrous was an eighty-six-year-old man with Parkinson’s disease, COPD, and other medical complications.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Decedent was admitted to Porter Medical Center on May 10, 2016, for increasing weakness, difficulty walking, and auditory hallucinations.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: While at Porter Medical Center, decedent became increasingly agitated and aggressive, removing his telemetry box and swinging at and hitting hospital staff.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: To address decedent’s aggression and calm him down for treatment, the hospital staff elected to use a chemical restraint known as Zyprexa.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Nurse Sanders contacted decedent’s daughter to gain approval for the use of Zyprexa in a 2.5mg dose, to which his daughter consented.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Around 1:38 a.m. on May 11, 2016, Nurse Sanders administered decedent’s first dose of Zyprexa.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Around 8:00 a.m., decedent’s oxygen-saturation level dropped to fifty-nine percent. He was intubated and subsequently transferred to UVM Medical Center for respiratory distress.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Decedent died on May 14, 2016. His cause of death was believed to be septic shock related to aspiration pneumonia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Both parties presented expert testimony regarding the standard of care for use of chemical restraints.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court concluded that there was no undisputed evidence regarding the specific standard of care applicable to decedent’s circumstances.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The law favors upholding jury verdicts.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The trial court must view the evidence in the light most favorable to the jury verdict.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff has the burden of proving the applicable standard of care, that defendant breached that standard, and that as a proximate result plaintiff suffered injuries.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The jury heard multiple different versions of each element of the alleged standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff failed to meet the burden of proof for establishing the applicable standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The court acted within its discretion in denying a new trial given the state of the record.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Admitted for increasing weakness, difficulty walking, and auditory hallucinations. Symptoms possibly due to adverse interaction of Parkinson's medications.

focused_exam.neurological: Acutely delirious, agitated and aggressive behavior

requestables.name: Neurology Consult
requestables.description: Consult to evaluate potential adverse drug interaction

timeline_phases.name: Initial Management
timeline_phases.description: Management of acute delirium and aggression

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with acute delirium and aggression
decision_points.prompt: How should the patient's aggression be managed?

options.description: Administer Zyprexa as needed after assessment
options.clinical_reasoning: Zyprexa was administered without proper assessment

options.description: Conduct thorough assessment before administering any chemical restraint
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires assessment before chemical restraint

expected_action_court.description: Assessment should have been conducted before administering Zyprexa
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for assessment

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to assess before administering chemical restraint
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care not established by plaintiff

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed respiratory distress and was intubated
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Death due to septic shock related to aspiration pneumonia

key_findings: Plaintiff failed to prove the standard of care
Jury found no breach of standard of care

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to establish standard of care

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to Porter Medical Center

diagnoses.diagnosis: Septic shock related to aspiration pneumonia

allegations.allegation: Failure to assess before administering Zyprexa

court_findings.finding: Plaintiff failed to prove the standard of care
